title: Poteranthera pauciflora
intro: Poteranthera pauciflora, also known as the few-flowered poteranthera, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions, such as digestive issues and skin problems. Bioactive constituents of Poteranthera pauciflora include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Poteranthera pauciflora is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Poteranthera pauciflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a member of the genus Poteranthera, which is a group of plants closely related to the genus Silene. Poteranthera pauciflora is native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern Alps. The plant is often found in rocky and alpine habitats, where it grows as a low-growing, spreading species. This classification makes Poteranthera pauciflora a part of the larger family of pink, white, and purple flowers commonly known as carnation and pinks.
